human_verification.notes: Reviewed the housing-real-estate-development-and-community-development development-reporting package for reporting-boundary quality, authority-source quality, metric-definition handling, source-lineage discipline, IDIS/CAPER and CPD evidence handling, environmental review gate coverage, affordability and funding caveats, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to housing development reporting support, source tie-outs, metric-definition handling, disclosure caveats, and escalation for HUD CPD, IDIS, CAPER, HOME, CDBG, environmental review, LIHTC, funding, permit, lease-up, lender, investor, and benchmark conflicts.

Authority sources cover eCFR CPD, CDBG, HOME, environmental review, federal award controls, HUD IDIS, HUD CPD monitoring, IRS LIHTC monitoring guidance, ULI real estate development workflow, and GRESB real estate benchmark reporting with refresh and decay rules.

Evaluation scenarios cover disputed metrics, stale or conflicting records, incomplete submission inputs, environmental prerequisite gaps, generic-industry overgeneralization, unsupported claims, specialized compliance routing, missing tenant facts, conflicting policies, and tenant-specific adaptations.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/housing-real-estate-development-and-community-development/development-reporting-specialist/ to keep this overlay in the canonical NAICS-derived path.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated housing, real estate development, and community development reporting overlay where legal, compliance, environmental review, tax, appraisal, financial, lender, investor, and final submission authority remain external.
The specialist is intentionally collaboration-oriented and returns to the orchestrator whenever missing prerequisites, source conflicts, or authority shifts prevent safe reporting.
